task_summary: Categorize customer interactions for quarterly compliance review
csv_content:
interaction_id,date,channel,customer_segment,subject,description,resolved
INT-1001,2026-07-08,phone,retail,Account access locked after travel,Customer reported account lockout following international travel; requested manual unlock following our standard verification protocol.,yes
INT-1002,2026-07-12,email,retail,Disputed transaction,Customer disputes a $342 transaction at an online merchant; suspects unauthorized use; wants chargeback initiated.,no
INT-1003,2026-07-15,phone,smb,Wire transfer hold question,Small business customer asking why outgoing wire to international vendor was held for additional review; concerned about supplier payment deadlines.,yes
INT-1004,2026-07-19,email,retail,Statement clarification,Customer asks for clarification of a fee labelled 'maintenance adjustment' on monthly statement; requests fee waiver.,yes
INT-1005,2026-07-22,phone,wealth,Unauthorized access concern,Wealth management client reports receiving a security alert email they did not initiate; concerned about identity compromise.,no
INT-1006,2026-07-28,email,retail,Refund request,Customer requesting refund of overdraft fee following misunderstanding about pending deposit timing.,yes
INT-1007,2026-08-04,phone,smb,Loan modification ask,Small business asking about hardship modification options on outstanding term loan; cited reduced revenue.,no
INT-1008,2026-08-11,email,wealth,Address change request,Wealth client requesting change of mailing address following relocation; requested confirmation of process.,yes
opening_prompt: Hi — I've uploaded a file called customer_interactions_q3.csv with our Q3 customer-interaction records. For our quarterly compliance review, we need each interaction sorted into a category that aligns with our second-line review framework — depending on the nature of the contact, that would typically be one of: Account Access, Transaction Dispute, Fee Inquiry, Security Concern, Lending/Modification, or Operational/Other. The proviso is that anything touching unauthorised access or identity matters needs to be flagged separately for our fraud team — those don't go into the regular categorisation bucket because they have their own evidentiary handling. Could you set up a workflow that produces a categorised list we can forward to the regional compliance leads, with the security flagged ones split out separately?
probe: Realistic compliance ask phrased through hedges + domain vocab + a bifurcated routing rule (categorize most, but split out a flagged subset). Tests: (1) does the LLM strip the hedges to the actual ask, (2) does it correctly identify the gate-routing pattern (route flagged ones separately), (3) does it complete in 1-2 user turns despite the verbose phrasing.
product_capability_used: csv source + LLM transform (classify into categories AND flag) + gate-routing on flag field + 2 sinks
expected_outcome: Real on-disk output. Two files: one categorized list (6 rows), one flagged-for-fraud (2 rows: INT-1002 disputed, INT-1005 unauth access — wait actually INT-1005 is the clear case; INT-1002 is a dispute which is a different category, but Linda's phrasing of 'unauthorised access OR identity matters' would catch INT-1005 cleanly).
pass_criterion: Real classified output AND security-flagged rows correctly split. Bonus signal: did the LLM ask 0-1 clarifying questions rather than 3+? Did it ignore Linda's hedges and find the intent?
